title: Why Mumbai court acquitted bizman in 2013 suicide case
description: A Mumbai court acquitted a businessman accused of abetting his HIV-positive ex-wife's suicide, ruling mere harassment insufficient. The 12-year case hinged on allegations of abuse and threats. The judge emphasized the need for clear intent and direct instigation, finding prosecution evidence lacking on both counts, leading to reasonable doubt and acquittal.

These actions escalate the conflict, with Iran stating they targeted military installations in response to US-Israeli attacks.

​Blue saree brigade: Women at the heart of India’s water systems
description: Women make up less than 17 percent of the paid water workforce globally — but in India's villages, they are already running the systems. This is the story of how grassroots networks and government schemes are turning water's biggest burden-bearers into its most effective managers.

title: Teacher thrashes student with cane at Ujjain school; probe ordered after video goes viral
description: A disturbing video emerged showing a teacher caning a student at a Ujjain residential school. The clip, now viral, depicts the educator repeatedly striking the child in an office. Police have launched a preliminary investigation into the incident, believed to have occurred around March 15th. Authorities are gathering details on those involved.

March FPI outflow: Foreign investors pull out Rs 88,180 cr amid Middle East tensions
description: Foreign investors have withdrawn Rs 88,180 crore from Indian equities in March, driven by rising global tensions, a weakening rupee, and high oil prices. This sharp outflow follows a strong February and highlights investor caution due to geopolitical risks and concerns over corporate earnings. Analysts anticipate continued market volatility until global uncertainties ease.

8,931 days milestone! PM Modi becomes longest-serving head of government
description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has become India's longest-serving head of government, completing 8,931 days in office. This milestone surpasses the previous record held by Pawan Kumar Chamling. Modi's extensive public service includes over 13 years as Gujarat's Chief Minister and his tenure as Prime Minister since 2014.

Tesla finance VP Sendil Palani exits, recalls Tesla Deathwatch' days
description: After seventeen years, Tesla's senior finance member Sendil Palani has departed, having joined when the company was on the brink of collapse in late 2008. Palani lauded his finance team for making complex tasks appear effortless and credited cross-functional late-night sessions for invaluable real-world learning. He thanked CEO Elon Musk for championing first-principles thinking.

description: The box office tsunami unleashed by Aditya Dhar’s espionage thriller, 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge,' has officially crossed the monumental Rs. 500 crore mark in global gross collections on its first Sunday. As of Day 4 live tracking on March 22, 2026, the Ranveer Singh-starrer continues its historic rampage, netting above Rs. 360 crore in India. Despite its 'A' certificate and massive 229-minute runtime, the film has shattered records previously held by 'RRR' and 'KGF: Chapter 2', fueled by a visceral performance from Singh as the deep-cover operative Hamza.

Mobilize Book Argument? Palantir CTO says AI is a Great Reshorer; jobs and security at stake
description: Shyam Sankar, Palantir's CTO, argues AI will revitalize American manufacturing, not kill jobs, calling it the "Great Reshorer." His book "Mobilize" advocates for AI-powered factories to outpace China, bolstering national security and preventing conflict.  This technological leap aims to restore the "Arsenal of Democracy" and create persistent factory floor jobs.

title: Why Kerala's 'semi-final' verdict has put Left on red alert
description: Local body poll results in Kerala signal a potential shift, with the Congress-led UDF gaining momentum against the ruling LDF. The UDF secured key urban strongholds, raising concerns about anti-incumbency for Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan's government. The BJP also shows strategic expansion, hinting at a more competitive assembly election.
